summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that Glances started in web mode without API authentication exposes raw secret-bearing configuration data through /api/4/config and per-item config endpoints.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction pipeline should start the vulnerable Glances web API without authentication and prove that an unauthenticated caller can retrieve crafted secret values from /api/4/config and /api/4/config/{section}/{item}.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh cloned commit e6bce5bc7c72dc79c6fddd29303fed620b55d77c, built the Docker image, started Glances with a secret-bearing config, and printed EVOHUNT_SECRET_LEAK_CONFIRMED after verifying exact values for outputs.jwt_secret_key, outputs.ssl_keyfile_password, passwords.default, influxdb2.token, influxdb2.password, and ip.public_password.
verification_method: reproduction/verify.py waits for the local REST server, requests /api/4/config plus per-item /api/4/config/{section}/{item} endpoints, and asserts the responses contain exact expected secret values rather than a generic startup success or unrelated API response.
evidence_produced_by_run: ./reproduction/run.sh printed EVOHUNT_SECRET_LEAK_CONFIRMED from the containerized verification step.
The verification helper emitted JSON evidence listing the leaked config sections and exact secret items proven readable over the REST API.

Successful per-item fetches for outputs.jwt_secret_key, outputs.ssl_keyfile_password, passwords.default, influxdb2.token, influxdb2.password, and ip.public_password demonstrated direct secret disclosure.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host into a temporary workspace under the finding directory, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit or ref, and mounts that checkout into the container.
